'''Frank-Paul Nu'uausala''' (born 13 February 1987), also known by the [[nickname]]s of '''"Frank Paul the Wrecking Ball"''' or '''"Frank the tank"''',<ref>{{cite news| url=http://news.theage.com.au/breaking-news-sport/fatfree-frankpaul-ready-to-fire-20090404-9sa0.html| location=Melbourne| work=The Age| first=Todd| last=Balym| title=Fat-free Frank-Paul ready to fire| date=4 April 2009}}</ref> is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international representative forward, Nu'uausala most notably played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] with whom he won the [[2013 NRL Premiership]].

==Background==
Born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]], Nu'uausala comes from a large family of 6 sisters and 6 brothers, he is the fifth eldest and is of [[Chinese people|Chinese]], Maori [[Samoans|Samoan]] and [[Solomon Islands|Solomon Islander]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|author=Jamie Pandaram|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/nuuausalas-wreaking-destruction-but-now-its-on-the-field-20100723-10oph.html|title=Frank-Paul Nu'uausala speaks about his gang background|date=23 July 2010 |publisher=Smh.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref>

Nu'uausala has played rugby league since the age of 5, originally with [[Mangere East Hawks]]. Nu'uausala played for the [[Otahuhu Leopards|Otahuhu-Ellerslie Leopards]] in the [[2004 Bartercard Cup]] and toured England with [[New Zealand Residents rugby league team|New Zealand 'A']].<ref>Coffey, John and Bernie Wood ''Auckland, 100 years of rugby league, 1909-2009'', 2009. {{ISBN|978-1-86969-366-4}}, p.341.</ref> Nu'uausala was signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]] when he was 14 and played in their lower grades until 2006 when he was cut by incoming coach [[Ivan Cleary]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/league/news/article.cfm?c_id=79&objectid=10602500|title=League: Warriors reject who battled back - Rugby League - NZ Herald News|publisher=Nzherald.co.nz|date=2009-10-11|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> Nu'uausala later signed with the [[Sydney Roosters]].

==Playing career==

===2007===
In round 7 of the [[2007 NRL season]] against the [[Parramatta Eels]], Nu’uausala made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[Sydney Roosters]] of the interchange bench in the Roosters 16–36 loss at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/lhqmatchreport/wing-jeered-as-eels-ruffle-feathers/2007/06/23/1182019436425.html|title=Wing jeered as Eels ruffle feathers|date=23 June 2007 |publisher=smh.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> Nu’uausala finished his debut year in the NRL with him playing in 3 matches for the Roosters in the 2007 NRL season.

===2008===
In round 5 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Nu’uausala first match for the Roosters in 2008, scoring his first NRL career try in the Roosters 28–12 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.thefanatics.com/sports.news.view.php?id=474828|title=Roosters outclass Panthers 28-12 - Sports News - Fanatics - the world's biggest events|publisher=Fanatics|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> Nu’uausala finished the [[2008 NRL season]] with him playing 11 matches and scoring a try for the Roosters.

===2009===
Nu’uausala cemented a spot in the forwards in the Roosters squad playing in 24 matches for the Roosters in the [[2009 NRL season]] as the club finished last on the table for the first time since 1966. Nu'uausala was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] squad for the [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|2009 Four Nations]]. Nu'uausala made his international test debut against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], scoring a try in the 20–20 draw against Australia at [[The Stoop]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8319656.stm|title=BBC SPORT | Rugby League | Australia 20-20 New Zealand|publisher=BBC News|date=2009-10-24|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/the-wooden-spoon-to-premier-gold-roosters-run-recalls-league-history-20100921-15lfu.html|title=The wooden spoon to premier gold? Roosters run recalls league history|first=Chris|last=Barrett|date=21 September 2010|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

===2010===
For the [[2010 Anzac Test]], Nu'uausala was selected to play for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at {{rlp|pr}} in the Kiwis 12–8 loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at [[AAMI Park]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/kangaroos-v-new-zealand-trans-tasman-test-at-aami-park-in-melbourne/story-e6frep5x-1225863712628|title=Kangaroos v New Zealand Trans-Tasman Test at AAMI Park in Melbourne|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> In Round 12 against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], Nu’uausala scored a double in the Roosters 30–16 win at [[Robina Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://m.nrl.com/roosters-crow-in-30-16-win-over-titans/tabid/10874/newsid/58861/default.aspx|title=Roosters crow in 30-16 win over Titans - NRL.com|publisher=M.nrl.com|date=2010-05-31|access-date=2015-11-26|archive-date=2 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150402164419/http://m.nrl.com/roosters-crow-in-30-16-win-over-titans/tabid/10874/newsid/58861/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 3 October 2010, in the Roosters [[2010 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]], Nu’uausala played off the interchange bench in the Roosters 32–8 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters-lead-the-dragons-8-6-at-halftime-in-the-nrl-grand-final/story-e6frexnr-1225933487068|title=Roosters Lead the Dragons 8-6 at Half Time in the NRL Grand Final|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Glenn Jackson|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/drought-finally-broken-as-dragons-reign-in-roosters-in-wet-20101004-162kz.html|title=St George Illawarra Dragons | NRL | Premiership|date=4 October 2010|publisher=Smh.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> Nu’uausala finished the [[2010 NRL season]] with him playing in all the Roosters 28 matches and scoring 7 tries. On 5 October 2010, Nu’uausala was selected in the New Zealand [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations|2010 Four Nations]] squad,<ref>{{cite web|last=Johnstone|first=Duncan|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/4198067/Kiwi-squad-for-Four-Nations-announced|title=Kiwi squad for Four Nations announced|date=5 October 2010|publisher=Stuff.co.nz|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> playing in 4 matches including playing the interchange bench in the Kiwis 16-12 Four Nations Final victory over Australia at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/kangaroos-crack-again-as-new-zealand-wins-four-nations-final-16-12/story-e6frep5x-1225953277821|title=Kangaroos v New Zealand Trans-Tasman Test at AAMI Park in Melbourne|publisher=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref>

===2011===
Nu’uausala finished the [[2011 NRL season]] with him playing in 19 matches and scoring 4 tries for the Roosters as the club missed out on the finals after finishing 11th on the table.

===2012===
Nu’uausala finished the [[2012 NRL season]] with him playing in 19 matches and scoring 2 tries for the Roosters as the side missed out on the finals for a second consecutive year.
[[File:Frank-Paul Nu'uausala 2013.JPG|thumb|right|Nu'uausala during 2013 World Cup]]

===2013===
On 9 July 2013, Nu’uausala extended his contract for a further two years, keeping him at the club till the end of the [[2015 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/roosters-resign-the-wrecking-ball-20130709-2pnii.html|title=Roosters re-sign the 'Wrecking Ball'|date=9 July 2013|publisher=Brisbanetimes.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> On 6 October 2013, Nu'uausala won the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]] with the Roosters.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/rolling-coverage-of-sydney-roosters-v-manly-sea-eagles-nrl-grand-final/story-fni3fbgz-1226733760880|title=Roosters v Sea Eagles Grand Final|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/74996/default.aspx|title=NRL Grand Final player ratings|publisher=NRL.com|date=2013-10-07|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> Nu’uausala finished the [[2013 NRL season]] with playing in 23 matches for the Roosters. Nu'uausala was selected in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]] squad, playing in 5 matches and scoring 3 tries including in a double in the Kiwis 48–0 victory over [[France national rugby league team|France]] at [[Parc des Sports (Avignon)|Parc des Sports]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/world-cup/shaun-johnson-stars-as-new-zealand-hand-france-48nil-hiding-at-rugby-league-world-cup/story-fnk3vz4p-1226751724778|title=Shaun Johnson Stars as New Zealand Hand France 48-0 Hiding at Rugby League World Cup|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> Nu'uausala played off the interchange bench in the Kiwis 34–2 loss to [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup final|World Cup final]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/world-cup-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/75602/default.aspx|title=World Cup final player ratings|publisher=NRL.com|date=2013-12-01|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref>

===2014===
In the Roosters [[2014 World Club Challenge]] match against 2013 [[Super League]] champions the [[Wigan Warriors]], Nu’uausala played at {{rlp|lk}} in the Roosters 36–14 victory [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Michael Chammas|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/world-club-challenge-roosters-run-riot-in-title-win-over-wigan-20140222-339ok.html|title=World Club Challenge: Roosters run riot in title win over Wigan|date=22 February 2014|publisher=Smh.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> In Round 25 against the [[Melbourne Storm]] at SFS, Nu’uausala played up his 150th NRL career match in the Roosters 24–12 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters-secondhalf-surge-enough-to-end-melbourne-storms-topfour-hopes/story-fni3gpfh-1227042534854|title=Roostersd SEcond Half Surge Enough to End Storm's Top Four Hopes|publisher=Heraldsun.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> Nu’uausala finished off the [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing in 26 matches and scoring a try for the [[Sydney Roosters|Roosters]]. On 3 December 2014, Nu'uausala signed a three-year contract with the [[Canberra Raiders]] starting in 2015 after being released from the final year of his Roosters contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/city-east/frank-paul-nuuausala-leaves-sydney-roosters-for-canberra/story-fngr8ice-1227143328552|title=Frank-Paul Nuuausala Leaves Sydney Roosters for Canberra|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/frank-paul-nuuausala-released-from-roosters-contract-to-join-raiders/story-e6frf3qc-1227143415580|title=Frank-Paul Nuuausala Released from Roosters Contract to Join Raiders|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref>

===2015===
In round 1 of the [[2015 NRL season]] against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], Nu’uausala made his club debut for the [[Canberra Raiders]] off the interchange bench in the Raiders 24–20 win at [[Remondis Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sharks-v-raiders-cronulla-a-long-way-off-the-pace-in-loss-to-canberra-short-of-highlights/story-fni3gol8-1227253693351|title=Sharks v Raiders; Cronulla a Long Way off the Pace in Loss to Canberra|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> In Round 7 against the [[Wests Tigers]], he scored his first try for the Raiders in their 30–22 comeback win at [[Leichhardt Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2015-raiders-post-30-unanswered-points-on-wests-tigers-for-stunning-win/story-fni3gmmu-1227310651926?sv=c0eeb40a67c8b2489e38e692ad68b9fd|title=Raiders Post 30 Unanswered Points on Wests Tigers for Stunning Win|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref> He finished off his first season with the Raiders having played in all 24 matches and scoring 1 try.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/frank-paul-nu-uausala/summary.html|title=Frank-Paul Nu'uausala - Career Stats & Summary|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2015-11-26}}</ref>

===2016===
On 24 June 2016, it was announced that Nu’uausala signed a three-year deal with the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. Nu’uausala joined the club with immediate effect and is expected to arrive in the next few weeks once he receives his visa. Wigan coach [[Shaun Wane]] said: "Frank-Paul is exactly the kind of player we want at Wigan". "He's a very tough player who really throws himself into the action and is used to winning". "He's also very experienced having played in the World Cup final, the Australian Grand Final and the World Club Challenge". "We’ve not had much luck with injuries this year and this signing is exactly the kind of boost we need, at this time, as we look to mount a serious challenge for trophies". "The Wigan fans are really going to enjoy watching Frank-Paul play for us." Nuuausala said: "I’ve achieved everything that I can in my career in the NRL and, whilst still in my prime as a prop-forward, want to experience the challenge of Super League. "It would be great to win the Grand Final over there as well and the Challenge Cup at Wembley and joining a strong and successful club like Wigan gives me that best opportunity. "I’m looking forward to pulling on the famous cherry-and-white jersey for the first time and playing in the uniquely passionate environment of rugby league in England."<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/raiders/wigan-warriors-sign-raiders-prop-frankpaul-nuuausala/news-story/d74c54aa1d1b5b978b5b322fb696484a|title = Wigan Warriors sign Raiders prop Frank-Paul Nuuausala|date = 23 June 2016}}</ref> Nu’uausala played in 7 matches for the Raiders in the [[2016 NRL season]]. On his home debut for the Warriors, he was sin-binned against their old rivals St Helens which immediately made him a crowd favourite.

His running game and powerful tackles helped Wigan lift the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] trophy with victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|work=The Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

===2017===
Nu'uausala starred in the World Club Championship win over the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] becoming one of only a handful of players who have won the trophy with two clubs in England and Australia.

He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|work=Daily Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

===2018===
Nu'uausala re-joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] on a one-year deal after being released from his contract at Wigan.  On 22 August, it was announced that Nu'uausala would be retiring at the end of the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/news/2018/08/22/frank-paul-nuuausala-to-retire-at-seasons-end/|title=Nu'uausala to retire at seasons end|website=www.abc.net.au|date=22 August 2018}}</ref>
